dimensions.note: Docs-only slice: README workflow example, README coverage-gaps/errorsByKind prose, the module-doc comment, and the manifest description. No credential, token, or secret material introduced; the report remains SDK-free pure data shaping over workflow artifacts.
No logging changed. The only .ts edit is a JSDoc module comment (lines 11-12) that adds the transient-network coverage bucket to the prose summary; no log statements added, removed, or altered.

No control flow touched. renderMarkdown/execute still filter errors by kind (network, auth_expired, access_denied, other) and the never-throws guard is unchanged. The docs now correctly describe the network bucket the code already emitted, closing a doc-vs-code gap rather than changing behavior.

Docs-only change needs no new tests; existing suite already asserts the network path (errorsByKind counts the network bucket and renderMarkdown emits the transient-network section), so the tests already back the claims the README/manifest/module-doc now make. deno test for this extension stays green.
Report-only extension: it renders collected workflow data and creates/updates/deletes no external resources, so idempotency does not apply. Unchanged by this docs slice.
No external API client; the report consumes swamp data artifacts only and validates each with safeParse before use. This slice edits documentation and does not alter any consumed contract.

No file handles, sockets, or temp files added; docs-only change introduces no new resources. The corrected workflow YAML example was validated in a throwaway workflow (schema check passed) and removed, leaving no stray artifacts.

manifest version bumped 2026.06.26.1 -> 2026.07.19.0 (report has no model.version to align). README workflow example corrected to the real schema (jobs array, task block with modelType @jentz/aws-default-sg-audit + methodName scan, reports.require); coverage-gaps and errorsByKind docs now include the network bucket the code emits; manifest description aligned. No real account IDs, IPs, or hostnames in source, README, or fixtures.
